items.prompt: Should people be encouraged to keep role expectations even when those expectations pressure them to hide important parts of themselves?

ChatGPT.reasoning: People should not be encouraged to keep role expectations that pressure them to hide important parts of themselves, as this can harm their mental health and authenticity. Encouraging openness fosters well-being and genuine connections.
ChatGPT.reasoningPreview: People should not be encouraged to keep role expectations that pressure them to hide important parts of themselves, as this can harm their mental health and authenticity. Encouraging openness fosters well-being and...

Claude.reasoning: No. Hiding important parts of oneself causes psychological harm and prevents authentic relationships. While some social roles require boundaries, pressuring people to suppress their core identity is harmful and unsustainable.

Gemini.reasoning: No. Encouraging people to suppress core aspects of their identity for role expectations can lead to psychological distress and inauthenticity. True well-being comes from embracing one's whole self.
Gemini.reasoningPreview: No. Encouraging people to suppress core aspects of their identity for role expectations can lead to psychological distress and inauthenticity. True well-being comes from embracing one's whole self.

Grok.reasoning: No, encouraging people to hide important parts of themselves for role expectations promotes inauthenticity and mental harm; balance and self-expression should take priority.
Grok.reasoningPreview: No, encouraging people to hide important parts of themselves for role expectations promotes inauthenticity and mental harm; balance and self-expression should take priority.

Gemini.reasoning: Encouraging people to suppress core aspects of their identity to meet role expectations leads to widespread psychological harm and reduced overall well-being. This diminishes individual and collective flourishing.
Gemini.reasoningPreview: Encouraging people to suppress core aspects of their identity to meet role expectations leads to widespread psychological harm and reduced overall well-being. This diminishes individual and collective flourishing.
